description:
You are a container-platform specialist who has just inherited a three-service micro-architecture that is behaving sluggishly.  
 A preliminary snapshot of each service’s application log is already present on the filesystem; your job is to create a concise
 diagnostics bundle that the SRE team can import into their dashboard.

 Target directories that already exist:
   • /home/user/microservices/service-a/
   • /home/user/microservices/service-b/
   • /home/user/microservices/service-c/

 Inside every service directory there is a single UTF-8 text file named logs/app.log
 that contains one log entry per line.  Each entry follows this **exact** format (no
 extra spaces, single space separators):

     YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ LEVEL [request_id] latency=<latency_ms> status=<http_code>

 Example:
     2024-06-20T09:00:01Z INFO [c142] latency=120 status=200

 Your tasks:

 1. Parse the three log files and calculate, **per service**:
      a. total_requests  – total number of lines
      b. avg_latency_ms  – arithmetic mean of the integer latency values; keep **at most
         two significant decimal digits** but remove superfluous trailing zeros.
      c. error_rate_percent – percentage of requests whose status is **not** in the
         2xx range, expressed with one decimal digit (e.g. 10.0).

 2. Create the directory /home/user/diagnostics if it does not yet exist.

 3. Write a CSV file called /home/user/diagnostics/microservices_report.csv whose
    content is **exactly**:

        service,total_requests,avg_latency_ms,error_rate_percent
        service-a,10,121.5,10.0
        service-b,6,99.5,0.0
        service-c,4,218.75,25.0

    • The header line must be the very first line.
    • The three data lines must appear **alphabetically by service name** as shown.
    • End each line with a UNIX newline (LF), no extra blank lines at the end.

 4. Demonstrate parallel diagnostics gathering.  In the background, launch three
    simultaneous commands that capture the disk-usage of each service directory
    (hint: `du -sk`) and append the results, **prefixed by an ISO-8601 timestamp**, to
    /home/user/diagnostics/disk_usage.log.  Required line format:

        2024-06-20T09:11:02Z /home/user/microservices/service-a 4
        2024-06-20T09:11:02Z /home/user/microservices/service-b 4
        2024-06-20T09:11:02Z /home/user/microservices/service-c 4

    • All three lines must be present when the commands complete.
    • The file may list the lines in any order.
    • The final numeric field is the size reported by `du -sk`; its concrete value may
      differ from the example above and does **not** need to be normalised.

 5. No additional output files should be created.

 The automated grader will verify:

   • /home/user/diagnostics/microservices_report.csv exists and exactly matches the
     required header and three data lines shown above (byte-for-byte comparison).
   • /home/user/diagnostics/disk_usage.log exists, contains at least three lines, and
     each of the three service directory paths appears once and only once.
   • No superfluous files were generated in /home/user/diagnostics.

 Take care to use shell features that guarantee the three `du` commands actually run
 concurrently (background jobs, `&`, subshells, etc.).  You do **not** need root
 privileges for anything in this task.
truth:
Pre-existing structure and contents:

 1. Directories:
    /home/user/microservices/service-a/
    /home/user/microservices/service-b/
    /home/user/microservices/service-c/
    Every directory already contains a sub-directory `logs/`.

 2. Log files and their exact contents (10 lines for service-a, 6 for service-b,
    4 for service-c):

    /home/user/microservices/service-a/logs/app.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2024-06-20T09:00:01Z INFO [c142] latency=120 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:02Z INFO [c143] latency=110 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:03Z INFO [c144] latency=130 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:04Z INFO [c145] latency=125 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:05Z INFO [c146] latency=140 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:06Z INFO [c147] latency=115 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:07Z INFO [c148] latency=118 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:08Z INFO [c149] latency=121 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:09Z ERROR [c150] latency=119 status=500
    2024-06-20T09:00:10Z INFO [c151] latency=117 status=200

    /home/user/microservices/service-b/logs/app.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2024-06-20T09:00:11Z INFO [d242] latency=100 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:12Z INFO [d243] latency=102 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:13Z INFO [d244] latency=98 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:14Z INFO [d245] latency=97 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:15Z INFO [d246] latency=101 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:16Z INFO [d247] latency=99 status=200

    /home/user/microservices/service-c/logs/app.log
    ------------------------------------------------
    2024-06-20T09:00:17Z INFO [e342] latency=210 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:18Z INFO [e343] latency=230 status=200
    2024-06-20T09:00:19Z ERROR [e344] latency=220 status=503
    2024-06-20T09:00:20Z INFO [e345] latency=215 status=200

 3. Expected artefact after successful completion:

    /home/user/diagnostics/microservices_report.csv
    ------------------------------------------------
    service,total_requests,avg_latency_ms,error_rate_percent
    service-a,10,121.5,10.0
    service-b,6,99.5,0.0
    service-c,4,218.75,25.0

 4. /home/user/diagnostics/disk_usage.log must exist and contain exactly one line
    referencing each of:
        /home/user/microservices/service-a
        /home/user/microservices/service-b
        /home/user/microservices/service-c
    (The numeric size field can vary and is **not** checked.)

 5. Permissions:
    All directories under /home/user/microservices/ are 0755 and owned by user.
    The grader runs as the same user and can create /home/user/diagnostics/.

 No other files are pre-seeded.
